# Requirements Quality Checklist: CSV Import Primary Path + EventCreate Format Adapter

**Purpose**: Validate that spec / plan / contracts / data-model are well-written (complete, clear, consistent, measurable, fully-covered) BEFORE `/speckit-tasks`. Unit-tests-for-English on the requirements themselves — NOT a verification of implementation behaviour.
**Created**: 2026-05-15
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md) · [plan.md](../plan.md) · [data-model.md](../data-model.md) · [contracts/](../contracts/)

**Note**: This checklist generated by `/speckit-checklist` after clarify session 2 + critique pass-1 + pass-2 closed all blockers (0 🎯 outstanding).

---

## Requirement Completeness

- [X] CHK001 — Are all 3 user stories (US1, US2, US5) acceptance scenarios fully enumerated, including primary + alternate + exception flows? [Completeness, Spec §User Stories]
- [X] CHK002 — Are the dropped user stories (US3 match-preview + US4 template) explicitly marked DROPPED with rationale + revisit criteria, so a future maintainer reading the spec doesn't think they were forgotten? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 3+4]
- [X] CHK003 — Are all 6 required columns for EventCreate format detection enumerated by name (Basic Info, Status, First Name, Last Name, Email, Attendee ID), or does the spec leave the set open-ended? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001]
- [X] CHK004 — Are PDPA-consent classification rules explicit for all three result paths (true / false / null) with case-sensitivity stated? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009]
- [X] CHK005 — Is the inline event-create modal's source use-case named (not just "the same use-case as /admin/events/new")? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003 + Plan §Project Structure]
- [X] CHK006 — Are all 3 new audit event types (`csv_import_error_csv_downloaded` / `csv_import_cross_tenant_probe` / `csv_import_event_mismatch_overridden`) fully documented with severity + payload + retention? [Completeness, contracts/audit-port.md]
- [X] CHK007 — Are migration files 0139 + 0140 documented with their full column lists (incl. the `attendee_fingerprint` addition from X-R2-1)? [Completeness, data-model.md §Migration order]
- [X] CHK008 — Are the dropped artefacts (migration 0141, `csv_import_refund_review_signalled` audit, `MatchPreviewResult` type, CSV template route + contract) marked DROPPED everywhere they used to be referenced, leaving no orphan mentions? [Completeness, Cross-file]

---

## Requirement Clarity

- [X] CHK009 — Is the EventCreate header detection's case-sensitivity stated explicitly ("case-sensitive exact match" vs "case-insensitive substring")? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001 + research.md R2]
- [X] CHK010 — Is the Notes→payment_status mapping written as a complete closed table with every recognized value enumerated, and the fallback path named? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008 + research.md R5]
- [X] CHK011 — Is the rollback trigger ("> 5 admin support issues attributable to F6.1 in first 7 days") quantified enough to be unambiguously evaluated post-launch (what counts as "attributable"?), or is it admin-discretion? [Clarity, Spec §Rollback Plan]
- [X] CHK012 — Is the `attendee_fingerprint` computation's input order (sorted? lowercased? Status filter applied?) specified deterministically enough that two implementations would produce the same hash? [Clarity, Spec §FR-019a]
- [X] CHK013 — Is the inline event-create modal's behaviour on validation failure specified (modal stays open with errors? closes and shows toast?), or left to implementer? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003]
- [X] CHK014 — Are the filename → event-name fuzzy-match thresholds quantified, or is "fuzzy-matches" left undefined? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004]
- [X] CHK015 — Is the `force_proceed` form field's accepted-value normalization explicit (`"true"` only? case-sensitivity? other truthy values?), or could implementations diverge? [Clarity, contracts/csv-import-eventcreate-api.md]

---

## Requirement Consistency

- [X] CHK016 — Does the audit event count match across all references (spec FR-024 "3 new" / plan Constitution Check "3 new = 14 total" / audit-port.md "Total after merge: 14")? [Consistency, Cross-file]
- [X] CHK017 — Are the migration numbers consistent (0139 + 0140 only, no residual 0141 references) across plan.md, data-model.md, quickstart.md, research.md? [Consistency, Cross-file]
- [X] CHK018 — Does the `event_registrations.attendee_pdpa_consent_acknowledged` column type (BOOLEAN per Q1) match between spec FR-009, data-model migration 0140, and any audit-port payload type references? [Consistency, Cross-file]
- [X] CHK019 — Do `ImportCsvOutcome` discriminated-union variants enumerated in data-model match those documented in contracts/csv-import-eventcreate-api.md (no extra/missing variant)? [Consistency, data-model.md + contracts/]
- [X] CHK020 — Are the FR-017 idempotency-key hash (event_external_id NUL email NUL timestamp) and the FR-019a attendee_fingerprint hash semantically distinct + non-conflicting (different purposes — duplicate detection vs event-mismatch safety net)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-017 + §FR-019a]

---

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [X] CHK021 — Can SC-001 (full workflow < 3 min) be objectively measured, including which timestamps mark "start" and "end" of the 3-minute window? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]
- [X] CHK022 — Is SC-002's "100% of rows with Status=Attending land as registrations after a single Confirm click" testable in an integration test against the committed real-fixture CSVs? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002]
- [X] CHK023 — Is SC-008's recast measurement ("TSCC admin completes first import < 5 minutes, measurable from csv_import_records") objectively verifiable from data, or does it still require subjective judgment? [Measurability, Spec §SC-008]
- [X] CHK024 — Is SC-006 (1k rows < 60s) inherited from Phase 7 with the SAME prod-region operator-gate convention documented (no scope drift from Phase 7's "UNVERIFIED on cross-region" baseline)? [Consistency, Spec §SC-006]

---

## Scenario Coverage

- [X] CHK025 — Does the spec define the alternate flow for "admin uploads CSV but no F6 event exists yet" (inline modal create vs leave page + return)? [Coverage, Spec §FR-003]
- [X] CHK026 — Does the spec define the exception flow for "admin uploads same CSV to wrong event" (FR-019b warning) AND the recovery flow for "admin proceeds anyway"? [Coverage, Spec §FR-019b/c]
- [X] CHK027 — Does the spec define the recovery flow for "Vercel Blob upload fails after import commits" (R6 + critique E2 fix), specifically what the admin sees and how to re-attempt? [Coverage, research.md R6]
- [X] CHK028 — Are the cross-tenant probe scenarios specified for BOTH endpoints (POST import + GET error-csv signed-url)? [Coverage, contracts/csv-import-eventcreate-api.md + error-csv-signed-url-api.md]
- [X] CHK029 — Is the "admin cancels mid-import" scenario addressed (or explicitly excluded — e.g., "no graceful-stop in v1 per E14")? [Coverage, plan.md §Operational notes E14]

---

## Edge Case Coverage

- [X] CHK030 — Is the embedded-newline-in-quoted-cell parser relax (RFC 4180) covered by an explicit test fixture (e.g., the Grant Thornton CSV row 2 multi-line address) referenced by name? [Edge Case, research.md R1]
- [X] CHK031 — Are unusual EventCreate `Status` values (Waitlisted, Pending, custom strings) handled by an explicit policy ("skipped + reported") rather than implicit fallthrough? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-007]
- [X] CHK032 — Is the 30-day fingerprint window's edge behaviour (29 days vs 31 days since prior import) deterministic (cutoff is strict `> NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'`, exclusive vs inclusive)? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-019b]
- [X] CHK033 — Is the "admin uploads CSV with 0 Attending rows" edge case handled (fingerprint would be hash of empty list — still valid? skip the safety net?)? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-019a]

---

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [X] CHK034 — Is the performance budget for the FR-019b fingerprint lookup query specified (e.g., "p95 < 100ms on indexed `idx_csv_import_records_tenant_fingerprint_uploaded_at`")? [Performance, Gap]
- [X] CHK035 — Are i18n key estimates (~30 EN × 3 locales = ~90 entries) covered by an enumerated key-namespace plan, or just a count? [Completeness, plan.md §Constitution V]
- [X] CHK036 — Are accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA) requirements specified for the inline event-create modal AND the event-mismatch warning dialog separately from the base event-picker (3 visual states stated in plan.md)? [Coverage, plan.md §Constitution VI]
- [X] CHK037 — Are the 3 new OTel metrics + 1 alert + 1 pino aggregate log enumerated by name AND label dimensions (tenant / format / etc.)? [Completeness, plan.md §Constitution VII]

---

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [X] CHK038 — Is the assumption "EventCreate export format columns remain stable" documented as an explicit risk + mitigation (unknown-column tolerance via FR-012 + pino aggregate log)? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions + Spec §FR-012]
- [X] CHK039 — Is the F4 module untouched-in-v1 status documented explicitly so a future reader doesn't reintroduce the cross-cutting? [Assumption, data-model.md §Cross-feature]
- [X] CHK040 — Is the dependency on F7 broadcast's future consumption of `attendee_pdpa_consent_acknowledged` documented as "preparatory, not consumed in this feature"? [Dependency, data-model.md §Cross-feature]

---

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [X] CHK041 — Are there any remaining ambiguous adjectives ("smooth", "fast", "convenient", "intuitive") that lack a measurable definition? [Ambiguity, Cross-file scan]
- [X] CHK042 — Do any FRs contradict each other (e.g., FR-019 "re-upload always wins" vs the audit trail of overridden manual edits — is the audit trail itself enough or does the user-facing UX surface this conflict)? [Conflict, Spec §FR-019]
- [X] CHK043 — Are there any [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers remaining in spec.md or plan.md? [Ambiguity, Cross-file]

---

## Notes

- Pass criterion: each item is either checked OFF (requirement passes the quality bar) or commented inline with the gap + intended resolution
- Items 80%+ traceability — each has `[Spec §X.Y]` reference, `[Gap]` / `[Ambiguity]` / `[Conflict]` / `[Assumption]` marker, or `[Cross-file]` for multi-artifact consistency
- This checklist tests **the writing of the requirements**, not the implementation. Items that pass mean the spec is ready for `/speckit-tasks`, not that the code works
- If ≥3 CHK items fail with the same quality dimension (e.g., 3 Clarity gaps), pause `/speckit-tasks` and revise spec/plan first
- Categories chosen per `/speckit-checklist` standard: Completeness · Clarity · Consistency · Measurability · Coverage · Edge Cases · NFR · Dependencies · Ambiguities
